channel.title: #DigitalBlackOutIran
channel.description: Daily simulation of how much GDP the world and the top 10 economies would lose if their own internet were blacked out for as many days as Iran's.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 67
item.description: 2026-05-05 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $65.03B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 66
item.description: Day 66 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $18.90B/day; world $64.65B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 65
item.description: 2026-05-03 internet blackout simulation: day 65, world scenario $64.27B/day. United States leads at $18.79B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 64
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 64: world daily loss $63.88B, cumulative world loss $3.17T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 63
item.description: 2026-05-01 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $63.50B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 62
item.description: Day 62 blackout simulation report: United States $18.45B/day, China $9.91B/day, world $63.11B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 61
item.description: SEO update for 2026-04-29: simulated blackout losses show United States at $18.34B/day and world exposure at $62.73B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 60
item.description: Internet blackout day 60: modeled daily loss reaches $62.34B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 59
item.description: 2026-04-27 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $61.95B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 58
item.description: Day 58 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $17.99B/day; world $61.56B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 57
item.description: 2026-04-25 internet blackout simulation: day 57, world scenario $61.16B/day. United States leads at $17.88B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 56
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 56: world daily loss $60.77B, cumulative world loss $2.67T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 55
item.description: 2026-04-23 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $60.37B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 54
item.description: Day 54 blackout simulation report: United States $17.53B/day, China $9.41B/day, world $59.97B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 53
item.description: SEO update for 2026-04-21: simulated blackout losses show United States at $17.42B/day and world exposure at $59.57B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 52
item.description: Internet blackout day 52: modeled daily loss reaches $59.17B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 51
item.description: 2026-04-19 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $58.77B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 50
item.description: Day 50 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $17.06B/day; world $58.36B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 49
item.description: 2026-04-17 internet blackout simulation: day 49, world scenario $57.96B/day. United States leads at $16.94B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 48
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 48: world daily loss $57.55B, cumulative world loss $2.20T; United States remains the largest economy case.
